name: Pico Gilbo Circular Trail
name_original: Pico Gilbo (1.679 m) – trail running Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre
description: This 5.0-mile, difficult trail running route to Pico Gilbo in Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre offers 1663 feet of elevation gain
region_intro: Jogging around Embalse de Riaño offers routes through a landscape characterized by the expansive reservoir, surrounding mountains, and varied terrain. The region features a mix of forested areas, open hillsides, and paths along the water's edge. Runners can expect to encounter both challenging ascents and more level sections, providing diverse experiences for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Embalse de Riaño The most popular jogging route is Pico Gilbo Circular Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding peaks and the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirva-Rabanal Trail, a difficult 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This trail navigates through varied terrain, including sections with significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Hórreo of Salio - Riaño – Hoz Oscura loop from Riaño, a 13.9 miles (22.3 km) trail leading through traditional architecture and reservoir views, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Jogging around Embalse de Riaño is defined by reservoir-side paths, mountainous terrain, and forested sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain running routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. Nearly 300 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse de Riaño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Embalse de Riaño?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;13 curated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Embalse de Riaño on komoot, offering diverse experiences for different fitness levels. These include challenging mountain runs and more moderate loops along the reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Embalse de Riaño?
A: While many routes in Embalse de Riaño are rated difficult, there is at least one easy route available. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31877297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Swing loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.8 km long and offers a less strenuous experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Embalse de Riaño?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Riaño is varied, featuring reservoir-side paths, mountainous sections, and forested areas. You'll encounter a mix of challenging ascents and more level stretches, providing a diverse running experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Embalse de Riaño area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055978834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an 8 km loop offering great views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio - Riaño – Hoz Oscura loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 22.3 km route.

Q: What can I see along the running routes in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the reservoir and surrounding mountains. You can also encounter interesting landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio - Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Dam&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options around Embalse de Riaño?
A: While many routes are challenging, families looking for a shorter, less intense run might consider the 5.4 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir – Las Viescas Recreation Area loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, though it is rated difficult due to some elevation changes. Always check the route details for suitability.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Embalse de Riaño?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the stunning views of the reservoir and peaks, as well as the diverse and challenging terrain that provides a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through unique natural areas or forests?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through forested sections. You might also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6811232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Pine Forest with Wooden Masks&lt;/a&gt; an interesting point of interest near some trails, offering a unique natural and artistic experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Embalse de Riaño?
A: The best time for running in Embalse de Riaño is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer weather, especially at midday. Winter running is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions and potential snow in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any long-distance running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio - Riaño – Hoz Oscura loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 22.3 km route with significant elevation changes, typically taking over 3 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views of the Embalse de Riaño?
A: Many routes are designed to showcase the stunning vistas of the reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055978834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its views of the surrounding peaks and the reservoir. Additionally, specific highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Riaño Reservoir and Bridge&lt;/a&gt; offer excellent photo opportunities.
tips: The Mirador de Las Biescas, located near Riaño (León), is one of the most spectacular spots in the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park. It's ideal for a simple hike, with stunning views and a touch of legend.

Pico Gilbo is an iconic mountain located in the Cantabrian Mountains, near Riaño, in the province of León. With an altitude of 1,679 metres, it is known for its stunning panoramic views and challenging climb.

faqs.question: What is the actual difficulty of the Pico Gilbo trail, especially for trail running?
faqs.answer: While the initial sections might seem suitable for jogging, the Pico Gilbo trail is generally rated as difficult. It quickly transitions into steep, rocky terrain with loose stones, requiring good physical fitness and sure-footedness. The final climb to the summit involves very steep sections, some requiring the use of hands, and exposed passages. It's best suited for experienced trail runners or hikers rather than casual joggers.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park and begin this trail?
faqs.answer: The classic route for Pico Gilbo typically starts near Riaño. You'll find parking options in or around the village, which serves as a common trailhead for routes in the area. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for access to the mountain.

faqs.question: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular high-mountain landscapes. You'll get panoramic views of dense forests, lush meadows, and the striking turquoise waters of the Riaño Reservoir. From the summit, a 360-degree panorama awaits, encompassing the Picos de Europa, surrounding valleys, and peaks like Peñas Pintas and Pico Yordas. Don't miss the View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho and the Gilbo Peak itself.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Gilbo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. However, given the challenging and exposed nature of the Pico Gilbo ascent, with steep, rocky sections and potential scrambling, it might not be suitable for all dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are fit for such terrain and keep them on a leash, especially due to the presence of local flora and fauna.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to trail run Pico Gilbo?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle Pico Gilbo is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October) when the weather is more stable and snow has melted from the higher elevations. Summer offers longer daylight hours, but can be hot. Avoid wet weather, as the trail can become very slippery and dangerous due to loose stones and exposed sections.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, including Pico Gilbo. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding wildlife and environmental protection.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You may find yourself on parts of the Camino de las Viescas and the Camino Vadiniense, which are official trails in the region.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre is rich in biodiversity. While on the trail, you might spot various local flora and fauna. The area is known for wildlife viewing, including species like European bison and water buffalo in certain parts of the park, though direct encounters on the steep Gilbo ascent are less common. Always observe wildlife from a respectful distance.

faqs.question: Is Pico Gilbo suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its significant elevation gain, steep, rocky, and exposed sections, Pico Gilbo is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires good physical fitness, sure-footedness, and experience with challenging mountain terrain. There are more family-friendly and less technical trails available in the wider Riaño area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trail run on Pico Gilbo?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and exposure, you should bring appropriate gear. This includes sturdy trail running shoes with good grip, layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and a first-aid kit. Poles can be helpful for the steep ascents and descents. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is the trail well-marked, or do I need navigation skills?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally well-marked, but in some sections, you'll need to follow stone milestones. Given the challenging terrain and potential for rapidly changing weather conditions, it's advisable to have basic navigation skills and carry a map or use a GPS device (like the komoot app) to stay on track, especially if visibility decreases.

waypoints.name: View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho
